executive_summary: BrightLane should start with an internal meeting intelligence workflow that turns leadership calls into a reviewed summary package before expanding into customer-facing automation.

objectives.title: Automate recurring internal reporting first
objectives.rationale: The highest-confidence phase-one value is reducing manual weekly reporting work before holiday planning.

objectives.title: Keep human review for all distributed outputs
objectives.rationale: Leadership wants time savings, but not black-box or uncontrolled messaging.

objectives.title: Use auditable AI plus deterministic rules
objectives.rationale: The workflow should synthesize what matters while preserving traceability to source evidence.

action_items.title: Pilot the workflow on one recurring leadership meeting type
Add an approval step before Slack or Slide distribution
Prepare a rough monthly cost view for 20 meetings

next_steps: Run the workflow against three historical meeting transcripts.
Review objective/action quality with operations leadership.
Decide whether to enable Google Slides publishing after review.
risks: Transcript quality can weaken action-item extraction and evidence mapping.
Inconsistent input discipline will reduce trust in the output package.

evidence.quote: [07:31] Sarah: We have holiday planning starting in about three months.
I would say human approval is mandatory before anything goes out broadly.
In terms of next steps, I'd probably propose a small proof of concept around one recurring meeting type.
I would say human approval is mandatory before anything goes out broadly.
I'd also want some very basic cost estimate before we commit, even if it's directional.
